name: Vinegar + Iron Wood Stain
description: Iron acetate solution made by dissolving steel wool in vinegar, reacting with wood tannins to produce a natural aged/weathered finish — zero VOCs, zero toxicity, pennies per project.
shelfLife: Iron acetate solution: months in sealed jar
effectivenessNote: Produces beautiful, natural-looking aged finishes. Color depends on wood species and tannin content. High-tannin woods (oak, walnut) react dramatically. Low-tannin woods need pre-treatment with tea.
warnings: Leave jar lid loose during dissolution — gas produced
Different woods react differently (oak goes dark, pine stays light)
Pre-treat low-tannin woods with strong black tea for better results
Test on scrap wood first — color darkens over 24 hours
Steel wool must be real steel (not stainless, not SOS pads)
scienceNote: Acetic acid dissolves iron from steel wool, forming iron(II) acetate in solution. When applied to wood, iron ions react with tannins (polyphenols) in the wood to form dark iron-tannin complexes — the same chemistry that causes natural wood weathering over decades, compressed into minutes.
